pattern: Accumulate visit counts into a hash map keyed by every suffix domain, then emit each key paired with its total count.
hints: Each entry contributes its count not only to the full domain but to every parent suffix obtained by dropping leading labels.
Split each label sequence and, for each suffix starting at successive dots, add the count to that suffix's running total.
Use a map from domain string to summed visits, then format every entry back into the count-space-domain form.

edge_inputs.description: single three-label domain expands to three subdomains
several domains whose suffixes overlap and sum
a two-label domain yields exactly two entries
repeated full domains accumulate into one total
shared top-level label aggregates across different second levels
deepest three-label domains sharing a common parent

stress.description: random count-paired domains over a small label vocabulary so suffix totals collide
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    labels = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'com', 'org', 'net', 'mail', 'web']
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    out = []
    for _ in range(n):
        rep = rng.randint(1, 10000)
        depth = rng.randint(2, 3)
        parts = [rng.choice(labels) for _ in range(depth)]
        out.append(str(rep) + ' ' + '.'.join(parts))
    return [out]
stress.note: small label set guarantees overlapping suffix aggregation
